name: Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — running circular por los Valles de Benavente desde Morales de Rey
name_original: Peña Redonda y miradores del Coto y de las Suertes — running circular por los Valles de Benavente desde Morales de Rey
region_intro: Jogging routes around Morales de Rey offer diverse terrain for runners, situated in the province of Zamora, Spain. The region is characterized by the Eria River and its fertile lowlands, providing flat paths along the riverbanks. To the west, the Sierra de Carpurias presents opportunities for trail running with inclines and varied terrain, including elevations like El Teso and Peñaredonda. The broader area features rolling hills, cultivated fields, and diverse flora, including pine and oak forests, suitable for various running experiences. Best jogging routes around Morales De Rey The most popular jogging route is Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — running circular por los Valles de Benavente desde Morales de Rey, an 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete, offering views across the Benavente Valleys. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Peña Redonda – Las Suertas loop from Morales de Rey, a difficult 15.3 miles (24.7 km) path. This route features challenging terrain and expansive views of Peña Redonda and the Las Suertas area. Local runners also love the View of Castillo de Benavente – Plaza Mayor de Benavente loop from Benavente, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through Benavente, often completed in about 46 minutes. Jogging around Morales De Rey is defined by riverside paths along the Eria River, challenging trails in the Sierra de Carpurias, and open cultivated fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more difficult trail challenges. The routes in Morales De Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 125 runners have used komoot to explore Morales De Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Morales de Rey?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Morales de Rey, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Morales de Rey?
A: You'll find a diverse range of terrains. The Eria River and its fertile lowlands offer flat, serene paths along the riverbanks, ideal for relaxed jogs. For more challenging trail running, the Sierra de Carpurias provides inclines and varied terrain, including elevations like El Teso and Peñaredonda. Additionally, the broader area features rolling hills and cultivated fields for open-space running.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, the Sierra de Carpurias offers more demanding trails. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña Redonda – Las Suertas loop from Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 15.3 miles (24.7 km) and features significant elevation changes, providing expansive views.

Q: What are some good options for moderate-difficulty running in Morales de Rey?
A: Most of the running routes in Morales de Rey are classified as moderate. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026469023/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-running-circular-por-los-valles-de-benavente-desde-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — running circular por los Valles de Benavente desde Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.9-mile (14.4 km) trail offering scenic views across the Benavente Valleys.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes or places to explore nearby?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly categorized, many of the flatter paths along the Eria River's vega would be suitable for families. For a shorter, accessible run with historical interest, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castillo de Benavente – Plaza Mayor de Benavente loop from Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.5-mile (7.2 km) trail that takes you through Benavente.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see while running in Morales de Rey?
A: The region is rich in history and natural beauty. You can incorporate sights like the megalithic burial site &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5966274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de San Adrián&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Labradas&lt;/a&gt; into your longer runs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark. The traditional wineries carved into the Sierra de Carpurias also offer a glimpse into local heritage.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Morales de Rey?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026469023/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-running-circular-por-los-valles-de-benavente-desde-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — running circular por los Valles de Benavente desde Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route offering diverse scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Morales de Rey?
A: The region's fertile lowlands along the Eria River are particularly rich and green in spring and summer, making these seasons ideal for enjoying the scenic riverbank paths. The pine and oak forests also provide a refreshing natural aroma. However, the varied terrain means running is enjoyable year-round, with cooler temperatures in autumn and spring being very pleasant.

Q: Are there any running routes that start or pass through Benavente?
A: Yes, several routes are connected to Benavente. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castillo de Benavente – Plaza Mayor de Benavente loop from Benavente&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.5-mile (7.2 km) trail directly through Benavente. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.3 km long.

Q: Can I find routes that offer good views?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de Carpurias, with elevations like El Teso and Peñaredonda, provides opportunities for trail running with panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026469023/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-running-circular-por-los-valles-de-benavente-desde-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — running circular por los Valles de Benavente desde Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is known for its vistas across the Benavente Valleys.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Morales de Rey?
A: The running routes in Morales de Rey are well-regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 3.0 stars. Over 125 runners have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain that caters to different abilities, from serene riverside paths to challenging mountain trails.
tips:
The traditional wineries dug into the slopes of El Teso and Peña Redonda are located in Morales de Rey, in the region of Benavente and Los Valles, in the province of Zamora. These popular buildings were and continue to be places of leisure and gathering for local residents, in addition to being used for winemaking and storage.
Morales de Rey has the largest number of wineries and the best-preserved ones in the region. These wineries are a testament to the rich winemaking tradition of the area and offer a unique experience for visitors interested in the history and culture of wine.
Near the town of Morales de Rey is the top of Peña Redonda, located at 815 meters above sea level with wonderful panoramic views.

From the Coto and Las Suertes viewpoints you can see the plain formed by the Eria and Órbigo rivers near their confluence, a fertile plain with fruit and vegetable crops, poplars and riparian forest. In addition to the plain, if the day allows it, it is possible to see the Montes de León and the Cantabrian Mountains

The Balcón de Basilia is a viewpoint located in Morales de Rey, in the region of Benavente and Los Valles, in the province of Zamora. This spot is known for offering one of the best panoramic views of Morales de Rey and its surroundings. Traditionally, the town's young people used to gather here every April 25th to celebrate the Tortillero, a local festival.

The Balcón de Basilia is a popular destination for hiking enthusiasts, with several trails that allow you to enjoy the landscape and nature of the area. One of the recommended trails is the one that runs from Morales de Rey to the summit of Coto, passing through the Balcón de Basilia.
